item.title: Kodiak AI raises $100M at a steep discount, sending its stock tumbling 37%
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The company made a series of other announcements during earnings, including a new commercial contract, a pilot program in Canada, and a collaboratio

item.title: Disney looking to make a unified ‘super app,’ report says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro, who took over for Bob Iger earlier this year, has emphasized his intent to streamline the Disney experience.

item.title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: AI startup Pit is led by the cofounders of European scooter giant Voi and backed by a16z, which is leading the startup’s $16 million seed round.

item.title: Hackers deface school login pages after claiming another Instructure hack
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai, Zack Whittaker
item.description: The cybercrime group ShinyHunters claimed to have hacked Instructure again, defacing the login pages of several Instructure customer schools with an extortion message.

item.title: Gusto hits $1B revenue, a figure that brings it closer to public markets
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Gusto has shared an impressive milestone on its actual revenue, not an ARR number that estimates future income.

item.title: OpenAI introduces new ‘Trusted Contact’ safeguard for cases of possible self-harm
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The company is expanding its efforts to protect ChatGPT users in cases where conversations may turn to self-harm.

item.title: Tesla Model Y is first car to meet new US driver assistance safety benchmark
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The new benchmark rating applies to 2026 Tesla Model Y vehicles assembled on or after November 12, 2025.

item.title: Perplexity’s Personal Computer is now available to everyone on Mac
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Perplexity's Personal Computer brings AI agents to your Mac, and is now open to everyone.

item.title: Elon Musk’s lawsuit is putting OpenAI’s safety record under the microscope
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Elon Musk's legal effort to dismantle OpenAI may hinge on how its for-profit subsidiary enhances or detracts from the frontier lab's founding mission of ensuring that humanity benefits from artificial general intelligence.

item.title: Bumble is getting rid of the swipe, CEO says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Based on Whitney Wolfe Herd's past comments about Bumble's new direction, the company is expected to lean into AI -- Bumble is even working on an AI dating assistant called Bee, and the CEO has made many comments over the years about how AI will be "a supercharger to love and relationships."

item.title: Tome, another Goodreads book-tracker rival, shuts down
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Built on the back of the sizable and influential BookTok community -- creators who discuss and review books on TikTok -- the app offered readers a place to chronicle and rate their books, get recommendations, and even add photos of things like favorite quotes or memes, or share playlists that match the book's vibes.

item.title: Could Lovable’s automatic 10% pay raise be the cure for toxic cultures?
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Stockholm-based vibe-coding platform Lovable is offering employees an automatic 10% raise as a way to sidestep classic corporate politics.

item.title: Hackers hack victims hacked by other hackers
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: An unknown group of hackers is breaking into systems previously breached by the cybercrime group TeamPCP. Once inside, the hackers immediately kick out TeamPCP and remove its hacking tools from the victims’ systems.

item.title: Kalshi doubles valuation in 5 months, hitting $22B
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Prediction market startup Kalshi has raised a $1 billion Series F round led by Coatue.

item.title: Jeff Bezos rep leaves Slate Auto’s board
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Melinda Lewison's departure raises questions about Bezos' support and involvement with the startup at a time when the Amazon founder is now apparently focused on robotics at his new startup, Project Prometheus.

item.title: How Anthropic’s Mythos has rewritten Firefox’s approach to cybersecurity
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Security researchers at Mozilla say Anthropic's Mythos has unearthed a wealth of high-severity bugs in Firefox.

item.title: Google’s $9.99-per-month AI health coach launches May 19
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The Gemini-powered health coach will act as a combination fitness coach, sleep expert, and health and wellness advisor.

item.title: Google unveils Whoop-like screenless Fitbit Air
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The device comes with health and fitness tracking like 24/7 heart rate, heart rhythm monitoring with A-fib alerts, SpO2, resting heart rate, heart rate variability, sleep stages and duration, and more.

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27: A shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Startup Battlefield 200 applications are open, but only for three more weeks. Apply by May 27 for your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, $100,000 equity-free, and more opportunities for major scaling impact.

item.title: Police arrest SMS blaster crew that sent malicious messages to thousands across Toronto
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Toronto police said this is the "first known instance" of an SMS blaster being used in Canada.
